Footer.brandDescription: Secure, Simple, Powerful. SSP is a groundbreaking, open-source, self-custody, BIP48 multi-signature browser wallet for multiple blockchains with Account Abstraction.

Home.metaTitle: SSP Wallet — Secure, Simple, Powerful Crypto Wallet
Home.metaDescription: True 2-of-2 multisig crypto wallet. Browser extension + mobile required for every transaction. Supports Bitcoin, Ethereum, and 15+ blockchains.

hero.description: True 2-of-2 multisignature wallet using BIP48 derivation, requiring both your browser extension and mobile device for every transaction. Features WalletConnect v2, ERC-4337 Account Abstraction with Schnorr signatures, and 15+ blockchains.
hero.videoUnsupported: Your browser does not support the video tag.

features.description: Experience the perfect blend of security, simplicity, and power in one comprehensive crypto wallet solution.

0.title: Secure 2-of-2 Multisignature
0.description: Advanced BIP48 derivation with true two-factor authentication requiring both browser extension and mobile device signatures for every transaction. Maximum security through dual-device protection.

1.title: Simple Yet Advanced
1.description: Complex multi-asset multisignature technology made simple. Advanced BIP48 security and Account Abstraction delivered through an intuitive interface anyone can use.

2.title: Powerful dApp Interaction
2.description: Connect to thousands of dApps with WalletConnect v2 (Reown) support, EIP-712 message signing, and real-time communication across all EVM chains and DeFi protocols.

3.title: Multi-Chain Support
3.description: Support for 15+ blockchains including Bitcoin, Ethereum, Polygon, BSC, Avalanche, Base, and more. First wallet with native Schnorr multisignature Account Abstraction (ERC-4337) on Ethereum, enabling smart contract wallets without EOAs.

4.title: Zero Data Storage
4.description: Complete self-custody with zero server-side data storage. Private keys, seeds, and transactions remain exclusively on your devices using military-grade AES-GCM encryption and device fingerprinting for maximum security and privacy.

5.title: Buy, Sell & Swap
5.description: Buy cryptocurrencies directly with credit cards or bank transfers, sell crypto back to fiat currency, and swap between different cryptocurrencies seamlessly within the wallet. Powered by an aggregated engine of multiple providers for the best rates and options.

security.auditDescription: All critical components of the SSP ecosystem have undergone rigorous security audits by Halborn, a renowned blockchain security firm. Our commitment to transparency means all audit reports are publicly available.
security.bugBountyText: We offer rewards for responsible disclosure of security vulnerabilities. Help us keep SSP secure.
security.lavaMoatDescription: SSP Wallet integrates LavaMoat, a security framework that protects against supply chain attacks. LavaMoat wraps each dependency in compartments and only gives them access to what they need, preventing malicious code from stealing secrets or compromising your wallet.

0.title: Package Compartments
0.description: Each package runs in its own compartment, preventing unauthorized access to sensitive resources

1.title: Policy Enforcement

2.title: Supply Chain Defense
2.description: Protects against compromised dependencies attempting malicious actions

3.title: Runtime Monitoring
3.description: Continuous enforcement ensures security policies remain active at runtime

security.deterministicDescription: SSP Wallet features deterministic builds using Docker. This means identical source code produces identical binaries, allowing anyone to independently verify the wallet you download matches exactly what was built from our published source code.

Academy.title: SSP Academy
Academy.description: Guides, tutorials, and deep dives to help you master self-custody with SSP.
Academy.metaDescription: Guides, tutorials, and deep dives on SSP, multisig, security, DeFi, and more.
Academy.comingSoon: New articles coming soon — check back shortly.
Academy.categoryEmpty: New articles coming soon. In the meantime, explore related topics:
Academy.seriesMetaDescription: Structured multi-part series to guide you from beginner to advanced on crypto self-custody with SSP.
Academy.seriesIndexDescription: Structured multi-part series to guide you from beginner to advanced.
Academy.seriesEmpty: Learning paths are coming soon. In the meantime, browse articles by topic:

categoryIntros.multisig: Multisig wallets require multiple signatures to authorize a transaction. SSP enforces 2-of-2 on every signed action: the browser extension and the mobile SSP Key app must both approve. This page collects the deep-dives that explain how the model works, what attacks it stops, and where its limits sit.
categoryIntros.getting-started: Brand-new to crypto, or returning after a long break? Start here. These guides cover wallets, addresses, transactions, gas, mempools, and finality — the vocabulary you need before you touch real funds.
categoryIntros.security: Self-custody is hard because attacks are creative. This collection walks through the threats that actually catch users: phishing pages, fake support DMs, malicious dApps, lost seeds, and how SSP's 2-of-2 model neutralises most of them.
categoryIntros.how-to: Practical, screenshot-driven walkthroughs for the things SSP users do every day: sending transactions across chains, connecting to dApps via WalletConnect, swapping tokens, and recovering from a lost device.
categoryIntros.coin-guides: Each coin or chain SSP supports has its own quirks — UTXO vs. account-based, native multisig vs. ERC-4337, fee dynamics, finality times. This collection covers them one at a time.
categoryIntros.defi: SSP supports DeFi via ERC-4337 Account Abstraction on EVM chains. These articles explain what AA is, what dApps you can use safely from a multisig wallet, and how SSP keeps you in control while letting you participate in DeFi protocols.
categoryIntros.news-explained: Beyond the headlines: the regulatory shifts, exchange failures, protocol incidents, and standards updates that actually affect self-custody users — explained without hype.

Guide.metaTitle: Setup Guide - SSP Wallet | Complete Installation Instructions
Guide.metaDescription: Step-by-step guide to install and set up SSP Wallet and SSP Key. Complete setup instructions for secure crypto wallet with multi-signature protection.
Guide.videoJsonLdName: How to Install SSP Wallet and SSP Key
Guide.videoJsonLdDescription: Complete video walkthrough: install the SSP Wallet browser extension and sync the SSP Key mobile app for 2-of-2 multisignature security.
Guide.howToJsonLdName: How to Install SSP Wallet & SSP Key
Guide.howToJsonLdDescription: Complete installation guide for the SSP Wallet browser extension and the SSP Key mobile companion app required for 2-of-2 multisignature security.
Guide.heroTitle: How to Install SSP Wallet & SSP Key
Guide.heroDescriptionPart2: : install the browser extension AND the mobile app. Both devices are required for the secure 2-of-2 multisignature system that protects your crypto.

Guide.stepsSubtitle: Follow these detailed instructions to set up your secure crypto wallet

0.title: Visit Extension Store
0.description: Open your browser and navigate to the SSP Wallet Extension page for your browser (Chrome, Firefox, Brave)

1.title: Install Extension
1.description: Click 'Add to Chrome/Firefox' or 'Install' button. A pop-up window will appear asking if you want to add SSP Wallet. Click 'Add' or 'Install' to complete installation.

2.title: Open Wallet
2.description: Locate the SSP wallet logo in your browser and click on it. Follow the prompts to get started.

3.title: Create Password
3.description: Create your password. Please carefully read the SSP Wallet Disclaimer and acknowledge your agreement.

4.title: Create Wallet
4.description: Click 'Create Wallet' to initialize your new wallet.

5.title: Backup Seed Phrase
5.description: Click 'Show Mnemonic Wallet Seed Phrase.' Store the seed phrase securely and confirm that you have backed it up.

6.title: Finalize Setup
6.description: Click 'Create Wallet' to finalize the process. Your browser wallet is now ready!

0.title: Download Mobile App
0.description: Download SSP Key on your mobile device and open the application.

1.title: Start Synchronization
1.description: Click on 'Synchronize Key' to begin the setup process.

2.title: Set Key Password
2.description: Set an SSP Key password and confirm it. Then, click 'Setup Key.'

3.title: Backup Key Seed
3.description: Click 'Show Mnemonic Key Seed Phrase.' Store the key seed phrase securely and confirm backup.

4.title: Complete Key Setup
4.description: Click 'Setup Key.' SSP Key now serves as second authentication factor for your SSP Wallet.

5.title: Scan QR Code
5.description: Click 'Scan Code' to synchronize your SSP Key with SSP Wallet. Scan the QR code displayed on your browser.

6.title: Approve Synchronization
6.description: Click 'Approve Synchronization' and confirm with your Key password. You'll be notified of successful sync.

Guide.tipsSubtitle: Follow these important security tips to keep your wallet safe

0.title: Secure Your Seed Phrases
0.description: Store both wallet and key seed phrases in a secure, offline location. Never share them with anyone.

1.title: Use Strong Passwords
1.description: Create unique, strong passwords for both your wallet and mobile key app.

2.title: Keep Mobile Secure
2.description: Ensure your mobile device has screen lock and consider using biometric authentication.

Guide.successDescription: By following these steps, you have successfully installed and set up both SSP Wallet and SSP Key, ensuring a secure and seamless user experience.

Support.metaTitle: Support - SSP Wallet | Get Help & Technical Support
Support.metaDescription: Get comprehensive support for SSP Wallet. Find answers to FAQs, access documentation, connect with community, or contact our expert support team.
Support.heroDescription: Find answers to common questions, access our documentation, or get in touch with our support team.
Support.channelsSubtitle: Choose the best way to get help based on your needs
Support.faqSubtitle: Find quick answers to the most common questions about SSP Wallet

0.question: What is SSP Wallet?
0.answer: SSP Wallet is a true 2-of-2 multisignature system consisting of two components: SSP Wallet (browser extension) and SSP Key (mobile 2FA app). Both devices are required to authorize transactions, providing unmatched security for your crypto assets across 15+ blockchains.

1.question: How do I install SSP Wallet?
1.answer: You can install SSP Wallet as a Chrome extension from the Chrome Web Store. Visit our download page and follow our comprehensive setup guide for step-by-step instructions.

2.question: Is SSP Wallet free to use?
2.answer: Yes, SSP Wallet is completely free to download and use. We don't charge any fees for the wallet itself, though you'll still pay network transaction fees when making blockchain transactions.

3.question: What cryptocurrencies does SSP Wallet support?
3.answer: SSP Wallet supports 15+ blockchains including Bitcoin (BTC), Ethereum (ETH), Litecoin (LTC), Zcash (ZEC), Ravencoin (RVN), Dogecoin (DOGE), Bitcoin Cash (BCH), Flux (FLUX), Polygon (MATIC), BSC, Avalanche (AVAX), and Base. For EVM-compatible chains, we support all ERC-20 tokens and you can import your own custom token lists. Solana support is coming soon.

4.question: Where can I find the complete list of supported assets?
4.answerBefore: For a comprehensive and up-to-date list of all natively supported chains and tokens, please refer to our official
4.answerAfter: . This spreadsheet is regularly updated with new additions and contains detailed information about each supported asset.

5.question: Can I use SSP Wallet with dApps and DeFi?
5.answer: Yes! SSP Wallet features full WalletConnect v2 (Reown) integration, allowing you to connect to thousands of decentralized applications across all supported EVM chains. You can use DeFi protocols, NFT marketplaces, and other dApps while maintaining the security of the 2-of-2 multisignature system.

6.question: What are FluxNodes and how can I use them?
6.answer: FluxNodes are nodes in the Flux network that help secure and maintain the blockchain. With SSP Wallet, you can easily set up and manage Fluxnodes, monitor their performance, and claim rewards directly through the wallet interface. Fluxnodes require a collateral amount of FLUX tokens and provide passive income through network rewards.

7.question: Does SSP Wallet support fiat onboarding and offboarding?
7.answer: Yes! SSP Wallet supports fiat onboarding and offboarding through integrated partners. You can buy cryptocurrencies directly with your credit card, bank transfer, or other payment methods, and sell your crypto back to fiat currency. This feature is available in supported regions and requires KYC verification.

8.question: Can I swap cryptocurrencies within SSP Wallet?
8.answer: Yes, SSP Wallet includes built-in crypto swap functionality. You can easily exchange one cryptocurrency for another across supported blockchains through our integrated swap partners. The swap feature provides competitive rates and executes swaps securely while maintaining your private keys.

9.question: Can I export my transaction history?
9.answer: Yes! SSP Wallet supports CSV export of your transaction history. You can export detailed transaction records for accounting, tax purposes, or personal record-keeping. The CSV export includes transaction dates, amounts, addresses, fees, and other relevant details for each supported blockchain.

10.question: How can I request a new coin to be added?
10.answer: We encourage users to reach out to our support team or contribute directly on our GitHub repository to request new cryptocurrency support. We evaluate each request based on community demand, technical feasibility, and security considerations.

11.question: Is there a mobile wallet available?
11.answer: Good news! We are actively working on expanding SSP Wallet to mobile platforms. Currently, SSP Wallet consists of a browser extension (SSP Wallet) and a mobile 2FA app (SSP Key). A full mobile wallet experience is in development.

12.question: Can I use SSP Wallet on multiple devices?
12.answer: Yes, you can use SSP Wallet on multiple devices. To set up on a new device, you'll need to restore your wallet and key using the corresponding seed phrases. Each device requires proper setup and synchronization.

13.question: Is there a transaction limit with SSP Wallet?
13.answer: SSP Wallet doesn't impose transaction limits. However, you're subject to the blockchain network limits and your available balance. Some networks may have minimum transaction amounts or maximum transaction sizes based on their protocols.

14.question: Can I use SSP Wallet offline?
14.answer: SSP Wallet requires an internet connection for most operations including transaction broadcasting, balance updates, and synchronization between devices. While you can view some cached information offline, you cannot send transactions without connectivity.

0.question: How secure is SSP Wallet?
0.answer: SSP Wallet uses multi-signature technology with mobile-integrated authentication, providing two-key protection. We use cutting-edge encryption and don't store any of your data - everything is stored locally and controlled by you.

1.question: What is SSP Key and why do I need it?
1.answer: SSP Key is a mobile app that holds the second private key required for the 2-of-2 multisignature system. Every transaction must be signed by both your browser extension AND your mobile device. This ensures your funds remain secure even if one device is compromised. SSP Key is available for iOS 15.1+ and Android 7+.

2.question: Can I recover my wallet if I lose my device?
2.answer: Yes, you can recover your wallet using your seed phrase. Both your wallet and key have separate seed phrases that should be stored securely offline. Never share your seed phrases with anyone.

3.question: Does SSP Wallet collect my personal data?
3.answer: No, SSP Wallet is committed to not storing any data information. Your private keys, transaction history, and personal data remain entirely under your control and are stored locally on your devices.

4.question: What are deterministic builds and why do they matter?
4.answer: Deterministic builds ensure that identical source code produces identical binary outputs. SSP Wallet uses Docker-based deterministic builds that enable anyone to independently verify that the distributed extension matches exactly what was built from the published source code. This eliminates potential supply chain attacks and provides cryptographic proof of build integrity.

5.question: How can I verify the deterministic build of SSP Wallet?
5.answerIntro: To verify SSP Wallet builds: 1) Download the release files (Chrome/Firefox zips, SHA256SUMS, SHA256SUMS.asc) from

6.question: Who signs the SSP Wallet releases?
6.answerBefore: All SSP Wallet releases are cryptographically signed by security@runonflux.io using GPG signatures. Our public key is available on
6.answerAfter: for verification. The deterministic build process includes Docker-based isolated environments and comprehensive checksums (SHA256SUMS) that are also GPG-signed to ensure authenticity and integrity.

7.question: Can I use multiple SSP Keys with one SSP Wallet?
7.answer: No, each SSP Wallet can only be paired with one SSP Key. The 2-of-2 multisignature system requires device-specific synchronization for public nonces and chain data. Using multiple SSP Keys would cause synchronization conflicts.

0.question: Which browsers are supported?
0.answer: SSP Wallet supports Google Chrome, Brave, Firefox, and other Chromium-based browsers. You can download the Chrome Web Store version for Chromium browsers or the Firefox version directly from our GitHub releases.

1.question: I'm having trouble syncing my mobile key
1.answer: Make sure both devices are connected to the internet, the QR code is clearly visible, and you're using the latest versions of both the browser extension and mobile app. Try restarting both applications if sync fails.

2.question: My transaction is stuck or pending
2.answer: Blockchain transactions can sometimes take time depending on network congestion. Check the transaction status on a blockchain explorer. If using Ethereum, you may need to increase gas fees for faster confirmation.

3.question: How do I update SSP Wallet?
3.answer: Browser extensions typically update automatically. You can manually check for updates in your browser's extension management page. For the mobile app, check your device's app store for updates.

4.question: What is SSP Relay and how does it work?
4.answer: SSP Relay is an open-source relay service that acts as a bridge between your SSP Wallet and SSP Key. It provides market information, fee data, enables transaction synchronization, and delivers push notifications. The relay enhances the user experience while maintaining security.

5.question: Does SSP Wallet support Replace-By-Fee (RBF)?
5.answer: Yes, SSP Wallet fully supports Replace-By-Fee (RBF) functionality. You can modify transaction fees and even change recipients after broadcasting a transaction, giving you flexibility in managing your transactions on the blockchain.

6.question: What fees does SSP Wallet charge?
6.answer: SSP Wallet has zero fees - we don't charge anything for using the wallet. You only pay the standard blockchain network fees that go directly to miners/validators for processing your transactions. The wallet includes automatic fee selection to optimize your transaction costs.

7.question: Why do some transactions fail to construct?
7.answer: Some transactions may fail due to blockchain size restrictions and UTXO complexity. When you have many small inputs, the transaction size can exceed network limits. In such cases, you may need to consolidate your UTXOs or break the transaction into smaller parts.

8.question: Do I need to restore my wallet when changing devices?
8.answer: Yes, due to device-specific encryption, you must restore both your wallet and key when changing devices or making significant system changes. Keep your seed phrases secure and accessible for this process.

9.question: What happens if I lose my SSP Key device?
9.answer: If you lose your SSP Key device, you can restore it on a new device using your SSP Key seed phrase. Install the SSP Key app on your new device and use the seed phrase to recover access. You'll need to re-sync with your SSP Wallet browser extension after restoration.

10.question: Can I backup my wallet to cloud storage?
10.answer: SSP Wallet doesn't support automatic cloud backups for security reasons. Your seed phrases should be stored offline in a secure location. Never store seed phrases in cloud storage, email, or any digital format that could be compromised.

11.question: What's the difference between SSP Wallet seed phrase and SSP Key seed phrase?
11.answer: SSP Wallet and SSP Key each have separate seed phrases. The wallet seed phrase controls the browser extension component, while the key seed phrase controls the mobile 2FA component. Both are required for full wallet recovery and should be stored separately and securely.

12.question: How do I migrate to a new computer?
12.answer: To move SSP Wallet to a new computer: 1) Install the SSP Wallet extension on the new device, 2) Use your wallet seed phrase to restore the browser extension, 3) Re-sync with your existing SSP Key mobile app. Your transaction history and settings will need to be reconfigured.

13.question: I'm getting "Synchronisation with SSP wallet needed" error on mobile
13.answer: This error typically occurs when changing phones or when a blockchain is not yet synchronized with your SSP Key. To resolve: 1) Open SSP Wallet, 2) Switch to the blockchain you want to re-synchronize, 3) Click the burger menu (top right corner), 4) Select "SSP Wallet Details", 5) Confirm access with your password, 6) In the first row, find "Chain Sync with SSP Key" and click the eye icon to show the QR code, 7) Scan the QR code with your SSP Key and approve the chain synchronization.

14.question: I'm getting "SSP Key Public nonces do not match or SSP Key Public nonces are missing" error
14.answer: This error occurs when public nonces used to construct transactions are not synchronized between your SSP Wallet and SSP Key. To resolve: 1) Open the burger menu (top right corner), 2) Navigate to Settings, 3) Find the "Public Nonces" section and click "Sync Public Nonces", 4) Scan the QR code shown with your SSP Key, 5) Approve the request on your mobile device. Your public nonces are now synchronized and you can begin transacting.

fluxFoundation.metaTitle: Case Study: Flux Foundation — Self-Custody Treasury with SSP Enterprise
fluxFoundation.metaDescription: How the Flux Foundation moved from scattered multisig and custom scripts to SSP Enterprise — securing the Fusion bridge and Foundation treasury with M-of-N vaults across every chain they use.
fluxFoundation.subtitle: How the Flux Foundation moved from scattered multisig and custom scripts to a single self-custody platform — securing the Fusion bridge and Foundation treasury without giving up keys to anyone.
fluxFoundation.heroQuote: We needed full control of our funds, transparency for the community, and no black box MPC sitting between us and our own treasury. SSP Enterprise was the first platform we found that matches all three — across every chain we actually use.

before.title: Before: scattered multisig and custom scripts
before.intro: The Flux Foundation had been managing multi-party signing for years. The problem wasn't a lack of multisig — it was that nothing tied it together across chains, and the commercial alternatives all required a tradeoff the team wasn't willing to make.

0.title: Scattered multisig setups
0.description: Different multisig wallets across different chains, each with its own quirks, signers, and recovery procedures.

1.title: Custom scripts to glue it together
1.description: Internal tooling for transaction construction, signing coordination, and broadcast — maintained by hand.

2.title: No unified audit trail
2.description: Approvals lived in chat threads and shared docs. Reconstructing who signed what required digging.

3.title: No middle ground in the market
3.description: Custodians and MPC vendors meant trusting a black box. Off-the-shelf multisig tools were Ethereum-only.

decision.title: The decision
decision.intro: The team evaluated the alternatives and grouped them into three buckets. Every bucket had a dealbreaker.
decision.bucket1Description: Fireblocks, BitGo, and similar. Vendor holds part of every key.
decision.bucket2Description: Safe and the like. Ethereum-only, no native UTXO support.
decision.bucket2Verdict: We hold Bitcoin and Flux too.
decision.bucket3Description: Wallets without policy engine, analytics, or proper audit trail.
decision.bucket3Verdict: We'd be back to building tooling ourselves.
decision.conclusion: SSP Enterprise was the only platform that addressed all three at once: native multi-chain (UTXO and EVM), true self-custody with no vendor key share, and a built-in policy engine, analytics, and audit trail.

whatChanged.title: What changed
whatChanged.intro: One platform, every chain the Foundation interacts with, one consistent signing model. The custom scripts are retired. The chat-thread audit trail is replaced by attributable on-platform records. Treasury and bridge multisig live side by side under the same governance.

0.title: M-of-N vaults across every chain we touch
0.description: Bitcoin, Ethereum, Flux, and the EVM L2s — same platform, same mental model, same security guarantees.

1.title: Two devices per signer, no exceptions
1.description: Browser extension and mobile app required for every signature. A single compromised device cannot sign.

2.title: Audit trail that holds up
2.description: Every proposal, approval, and rejection logged with the signer and timestamp. Replaces the chat-thread reconstruction.

3.title: Self-custody, end to end
3.description: No vendor key share. No MPC infrastructure to trust. If SSP went away tomorrow, our funds remain recoverable from signer seeds.

fusionBridge.title: The Fusion bridge
fusionBridge.intro: The Fusion bridge is one of the most security-sensitive surfaces the Foundation operates — it coordinates value transfer between Flux and other chains, and a single signing failure could be catastrophic. It's also the workload where SSP Enterprise's model pays off most clearly.

0.title: On-chain multisig
0.description: Native multisig on every chain the bridge touches — no smart-contract custodian, no relayer that can stall or front-run.

1.title: Two-device signing per signer
1.description: Compromising one signer's laptop or phone is not enough to push a bridge transaction. Both devices, every time.

2.title: Per-signer policy controls
2.description: Limits and whitelists configured at the org and vault level, enforced before any signature is collected.

3.title: Auditable end to end
3.description: Every approval and rejection attributed and timestamped. Community accountability without giving up self-custody.

fusionBridge.quote: The Fusion bridge runs on infrastructure we control end to end now. Same security guarantees we have for our own treasury, applied to one of the highest-stakes workflows we operate.

cta.title: Same model. Available for your team.
cta.description: The platform the Flux Foundation runs the Fusion bridge on is the same platform available to any team that wants self-custody multisig without compromise. Free tier to start. Custom plans on request.

Enterprise.metaTitle: SSP Enterprise | Self-Custody Multisig for Business
Enterprise.metaDescription: Self-custody M-of-N multisig vaults for business treasuries. Policy engine, spending limits, two-device signing, role-based access. No custodians, no MPC.
Enterprise.heroDescription: M-of-N multisig vaults for your organization. Every signer uses two devices — browser extension and mobile app. No custodians. No MPC. No single point of failure.

fluxBanner.description: Securing the Fusion bridge and Foundation treasury across BTC, ETH, FLUX, and EVM L2s.

problem.title: The Problem
problem.subtitle: Businesses holding crypto today face impossible trade-offs

0.title: Custodial Solutions
0.problem: Trust a third party with your keys

howItWorks.title: How It Works
howItWorks.subtitle: True self-custody at enterprise scale — no compromises

0.title: Create Your Organization
0.description: Set up your organization using your SSP Identity — a cryptographic proof derived from your devices, not just an email.

1.title: Invite Your Team
1.description: Add team members by their SSP Identity. Assign roles: Owner, Admin, Member, or Viewer. Every member needs SSP Wallet + SSP Key.

2.title: Create Multisig Vaults
2.description: Configure M-of-N vaults for different purposes — operations, treasury, payroll. Choose which signers are required.

3.title: Sign Together
3.description: Propose transactions through the web app. Each required signer approves with both their browser extension and mobile app. Broadcast when threshold is met.

howItWorks.diagramSubtitle: Every signature requires two devices per signer
howItWorks.thresholdLabel: 2 of 3 threshold met — ready to broadcast

features.title: Enterprise Features
features.subtitle: Everything your organization needs for secure crypto management

0.title: M-of-N Multisig Vaults
0.description: Configure any signing threshold for your team. 2-of-3, 3-of-5, or any combination that fits your governance model.

1.title: Role-Based Access
1.description: Owner, Admin, Member, and Viewer roles. Control who can propose, sign, or simply observe transactions.

2.title: Two-Device Signing
2.description: Every signer uses both a browser extension and a mobile app. Two keys per person, M-of-N across your team.

3.title: Multi-Chain Support
3.description: Bitcoin, Ethereum, Litecoin, Dogecoin, Flux, Polygon, BSC, Base, Avalanche, and more. One platform for all chains.

4.title: Transaction Proposals
4.description: Propose transactions with full details. Team members review, approve, or reject. Broadcast only when threshold is met.

5.title: Complete Audit Trail
5.description: Every action logged permanently. Member changes, vault operations, transaction approvals — full accountability.

policy.title: Transaction Policy Engine
policy.subtitle: Granular controls that cascade from organization to vault to individual signer. Stricter policy always wins.
policy.tierFooter: Policies merge top-down — the stricter limit at any tier is enforced

0.title: Spending Limits
0.description: Per-transaction, daily, weekly, and monthly limits. Set in USD or native token. Aggregate and per-token controls.

1.title: Address Whitelist
1.description: Three modes: disabled, warn, or enforce. Label addresses, restrict by chain. Block unknown destinations entirely.

2.title: Time-Lock Delays
2.description: Configurable waiting period before broadcast. Trigger automatically above a USD threshold. Cancel before execution.

3.title: Admin Approval
3.description: Require admin sign-off on high-value transactions. Set a USD threshold — anything above it needs explicit approval.

4.title: Per-Signer Overrides
4.description: Set individual spending limits for each team member. Junior traders get tighter controls, senior execs get higher limits.

5.title: Velocity Tracking
5.description: Rolling 30-day spending windows per vault, per signer, and per token. Automatic enforcement with TTL-based cleanup.

6.title: Emergency Vault Freeze
6.description: Instantly freeze any vault with dual-device WK signing. Blocks all proposals until unfrozen. Incident response in seconds.

7.title: 3-Tier Policy Hierarchy
7.description: Organization defaults cascade to vaults and signers. Vault-level overrides refine the rules. Stricter policy always wins.

security.title: 5-Layer Security
security.subtitle: Defense in depth — every layer must be compromised for a breach

0.name: Device Security
0.description: OS-level security, biometric auth, secure enclaves on mobile

1.name: Dual-Device 2FA
1.description: Browser extension + mobile app required for every signature

2.name: On-Chain Multisig
2.description: Native Bitcoin multisig and EVM smart contract verification

3.name: Business Policies
3.description: Spending limits, address whitelists, time-locks, admin approvals, per-signer overrides, velocity tracking — 14+ configurable controls

4.name: Audit & Compliance
4.description: Immutable logs, login tracking, and compliance reporting

2.title: Investment Funds

3.title: Partnerships & JVs

chains.subtitle: One platform for all your chains — UTXO and EVM
chains.footer: More chains added regularly. All ERC-20 tokens supported on EVM chains.

InteractiveDemo.modalSubtitle: Full setup and transaction flow with true 2-of-2 multisig security
InteractiveDemo.walletSetupSubtitle: Create your browser wallet with secure seed phrase
InteractiveDemo.keySetupSubtitle: Set up mobile app with separate seed phrase
InteractiveDemo.deviceSyncSubtitle: Connect your browser and mobile securely
InteractiveDemo.walletStoreTagline: Secure, Simple, Powerful crypto wallet with 2-of-2 multisig
InteractiveDemo.installExtensionDescription: Download the browser extension from Chrome Web Store. Available for Chrome, Brave, and Firefox.
InteractiveDemo.agreeToTerms: I have read and agree to the Terms of Service
InteractiveDemo.createSecurePasswordDescription: This password encrypts your wallet locally. It never leaves your browser and protects your seed phrase.
InteractiveDemo.writeDownWarning: ⚠️ Write this down and store it safely!
InteractiveDemo.clickToRevealSeed: Click to reveal your 24-word seed phrase
InteractiveDemo.seedConfirmedToast: ✓ Seed phrase confirmed and ready to proceed
InteractiveDemo.backupSeedDescription: This 24-word phrase is your master key. Write it down and store it securely offline. This is seed phrase #1 of 2.
InteractiveDemo.walletSetUpWith: Your browser wallet is set up with:
InteractiveDemo.setUpKeyMobile: Set up SSP Key mobile app
InteractiveDemo.walletSetupCompleteDescription: Your browser wallet is ready! Now you need to set up the mobile app for the second signature.
InteractiveDemo.downloadKeyMobileDescription: Install the mobile companion app from App Store or Google Play. This provides the second signature for your transactions.
InteractiveDemo.secureMobileKeyDescription: Create a password for your mobile app and enable biometric authentication for enhanced security.
InteractiveDemo.generateKeySeed: Generate your separate mobile key seed phrase
InteractiveDemo.backupKeySeedDescription: This is a separate 24-word seed phrase for your mobile key. Both seeds are required for full SSP recovery.
InteractiveDemo.nextScanQrToConnect: Next: Scan QR code to connect devices
InteractiveDemo.mobileKeyCompleteDescription: Your mobile app is ready! Now let's connect it to your browser wallet for the 2-of-2 security.
InteractiveDemo.scanQrWithKeyApp: Scan this QR code with SSP Key app
InteractiveDemo.generatePairingQrDescription: Your browser wallet shows a QR code with SSP logo. Open SSP Key app and scan to connect securely.
InteractiveDemo.establishingSecureConnection: Establishing secure connection with mobile device...
InteractiveDemo.exchangingKeys: Exchanging encryption keys and public keys...
InteractiveDemo.scanningQrCodeDescription: The mobile app reads the QR code and establishes an encrypted connection with your browser wallet.
InteractiveDemo.devicesSecurelyConnected: Your devices are now securely connected
InteractiveDemo.devicesPairedDescription: Perfect! Your browser and mobile are now connected with end-to-end encryption. You can now send secure transactions.
InteractiveDemo.createTransactionDescription: Fill in the recipient address and amount. The transaction will require further approval from your mobile device.
InteractiveDemo.signatureOneCompleteCheckMobile: Signature 1/2 complete. Check your mobile device to approve the transaction.
InteractiveDemo.mobileConfirmationDescriptionBiometric: A notification appeared on your mobile device. Review the transaction details and approve with biometric authentication.
InteractiveDemo.mobileConfirmationDescriptionPassword: A notification appeared on your mobile device. Review the transaction details and approve with your password.
InteractiveDemo.transactionSubmittedDescription: Perfect! Both signatures collected and transaction broadcast to Ethereum. The network is now mining and confirming your secure 2-of-2 multisig transaction.
InteractiveDemo.fundsTransferredSummary: Your funds have been securely transferred using true 2-of-2 multisignature technology. Both devices were required to authorize and sign this transaction.
